title: Alfred University and Classiq Launch Joint Academic Quantum Computing Initiative - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Alfred University and quantum software firm Classiq launched a joint initiative embedding Classiq's high-level synthesis platform into engineering curricula to bypass gate-level circuit coding, targeting power-grid unit-commitment optimization and advanced ceramics/glass materials modeling across SUNY and CUNY.

insights.claim: Classiq's high-level functional synthesis platform lets students engineer quantum algorithms without gate-level compilation skills, using abstraction to lower the barrier to quantum workforce development.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Software abstraction that removes low-level expertise is Classiq's route to embedding in academic pipelines and future enterprise seats.
insights.quote: allowing students and researchers to engineer functional algorithms without deep low-level compilation skills
insights.locator: The academic collaboration deploys Classiq’s high-level synthesis platform

themes.theme: Applied quantum in energy and materials

insights.claim: The initiative targets the power-system 'unit commitment problem'—optimizing cost-effective generation schedules against fluctuating demand—as a flagship near-term quantum optimization use case.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Grid unit-commitment is emerging as a concrete quantum-optimization beachhead in the energy sector.
insights.quote: the most cost-effective generation schedules to meet fluctuating regional energy demands
insights.locator: Zhan’s core research focuses on power systems optimization

insights.claim: The work builds on NSF-funded grants and a 2024 program with Rochester Institute of Technology and ISO-New England exploring quantum optimization for wholesale electrical grids, showing grid operators are already piloting quantum paths.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Grid ISOs engaging quantum research signals a real, if early, demand channel.
insights.locator: The joint initiative builds upon Zhan’s previous National Science Foundation

insights.claim: A second research branch under S. K. Sundaram applies quantum algorithms to model molecular configurations and structural tolerances in advanced ceramics, glass, and manufacturing components.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Extends quantum simulation demand into industrial ceramics/glass manufacturing.
insights.locator: Concurrently, the initiative expands into solid-state physics
